Two classical machine-learning approaches were evaluated for predicting soil organic carbon (SOC) from VNIR spectra. First, an SVR (RBF kernel) model combined with SNV and Savitzky–Golay first-derivative preprocessing achieved good predictive performance (R² = 0.756, RMSE = 7.65, RPD ≈ 2.03), slightly improving upon the baseline PLSR model. The scatter plot indicated strong agreement with observations, although slight underestimation was observed at higher SOC values. A second approach using Ridge regression with Multiplicative Scatter Correction (MSC) preprocessing showed moderate performance (R² = 0.61, RMSE ≈ 9.70, RPD ≈ 1.60), providing more stable but less accurate predictions.
